description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing male figure in a traditional ancient Egyptian pose, with one leg forward and arms at his sides. The figure wears a short kilt and a headdress, and is supported by a vertical back pillar that extends from the base to the top of the head. The surface of the sculpture is weathered, with visible wear and some chipping, suggesting age and exposure. The base is rectangular and solid, providing stability for the upright figure. The coloration is predominantly a mottled brownish stone, with darker areas on the headdress and some surface patina.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a standing male figure in ancient Egyptian style, supported by a rectangular base and back pillar.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full frontal details of the figure, including facial features, headdress, and forward-stepping leg.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the arm, and the vertical back pillar.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back pillar, the figure's back, and the rectangular base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile of the head, arm, and the supporting pillar.
attribute_subject_matter: standing human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
